summary: Closed, unmerged joke/test PR that replaced the entire README.md with a single line, `hello :)`. It removed build instructions, tool requirements, project description, DOL hash information, and contribution/Discord information. The only human comment excerpt was `poggers`; there was no substantive review feedback.
what_changed: README.md was reduced from the project README content to a single line: `hello :)`.
Removed the repository description for the Super Smash Bros. Melee US decompilation.
Removed the note that the built DOL can be shifted for modding or research purposes.
Removed the target v1.02 main.dol SHA-1 reference.
Removed build prerequisites including devkitPro, Python3, and gcc.
Removed instructions for downloading/extracting GC_WII_COMPILERS.zip into tools/mwcc_compiler and running `make`.
Removed contribution information and the GameCube/Wii Decompilation Discord invite.
decomp_lessons: No reusable decompilation technique is evidenced by this PR.

The PR is useful mainly as a negative example: preserve README build instructions, compiler setup notes, hashes, and contribution links unless deliberately replacing them with equivalent documentation.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.substance: No actionable review or technical feedback.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that restored or changed README.md build instructions after PR #464.
Search for legitimate README updates involving devkitPro, GC_WII_COMPILERS.zip, or mwcc_compiler setup.
Check whether closed joke/test PRs are filtered from decomp knowledge records.
